articles.summary: European Commission President welcomes possible peace deal European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen on Sunday welcomed signs of progress in talks over a possible peace deal between the United States and Iran. We need a deal that truly de-escalates the conflict, reopens the Strait of Hormuz and guarantees toll-free full freedom of navigation. articles.summary: White House to announce Iran agreement on Sunday, says report The White House hopes to resolve remaining disputes with Iran in the coming hours and announce an agreement on Sunday, according to Axios, which cited a US official. The report said the two sides are working towards a memorandum of understanding and that President Donald Trump is prepared to reset relations with Tehran if it meets US demands regarding its nuclear programme. articles.title: Report: US-Iran deal includes 60-day ceasefire extension
articles.summary: Report: US-Iran deal includes 60-day ceasefire extension A proposed agreement between Washington and Tehran would extend the current ceasefire by 60 days and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, according to Axios, citing a US official. The reported deal would provide additional time for diplomatic efforts while reducing tensions in one of the world’s most important shipping routes. According to the report, the agreement would also allow Iran to resume oil sales and enter negotiations on its nuclear programme. articles.title: Shehbaz Sharif says Pakistan ready for next round of negotiations
articles.summary: Shehbaz Sharif says Pakistan ready for next round of negotiations Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said on Sunday that Pakistan hopes to host the next round of talks between Iran and the United States in the near future, as Islamabad continues its mediation efforts between the two countries. “Pakistan will continue its peace efforts with utmost sincerity and we hope to host the next round of talks very soon,” Sharif wrote on X. Pakistan has played a central role in facilitating diplomatic contacts between Washington and Tehran as both sides seek a negotiated resolution to the conflict.

articles.summary: Pakistan’s PM Shehbaz Sharif praises Trump’s role in peace efforts Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif congratulated US President Donald Trump on what he described as his efforts to advance peace through diplomacy and engagement with regional leaders. Sharif said the recent talks had enabled an exchange of views and helped move peace initiatives forward with the goal of achieving lasting regional stability. He added that Pakistan would continue its efforts “with all sincerity and dedication” and expressed hope that the country would host the next round of negotiations in the near future.
